items.article_h1: Hugging Face Publishes Physics-Informed AI Guide for Raw-to-Insights Medical Imaging Pipeline
items.article_standfirst: A new community guide on Hugging Face details a physics-informed deep learning workflow that converts raw imaging sensor data directly into structured diagnostic insights, targeting US healthcare AI applications.
article_body: Hugging Face has published a community-contributed guide walkthrough a physics-informed neural network pipeline called NV-Raw2Insights-US, designed to process raw sensor data from imaging systems into structured diagnostic outputs without conventional image reconstruction steps.

The approach embeds domain-specific physical models directly into the neural network architecture, allowing the model to respect physical constraints of the imaging system during training and inference. According to the post's classification, the work falls within the computer vision research track on Hugging Face, with the codebase available as an open-source community collaboration.

For builders working on medical imaging AI, the method could reduce preprocessing complexity and improve robustness by forcing the model to honor physical signal properties from the start of the pipeline rather than learning them implicitly. This is particularly relevant for teams developing edge-deployed imaging systems where compute budgets are constrained and end-to-end latency matters.
article_why_matters: Physics-informed neural networks bridge domain knowledge and data-driven learning, potentially improving generalization on medical imaging tasks where labeled data is scarce.
End-to-end raw-to-insights pipelines can eliminate fragile multi-stage preprocessing steps that often introduce bottlenecks in clinical deployment.
Open publication on Hugging Face lowers the barrier for researchers and engineering teams to replicate, adapt, or extend the approach in their own imaging stacks.
article_source_note: This brief is based on the Hugging Face blog listing page and associated metadata; the full article content at the target URL was not retrieved. Specific technical claims — model architecture details, training data, benchmark results, and deployment benchmarks — are not available from the captured source, so the description above reflects what is inferable from the post title and classification alone.

items.article_h1: OpenAI Achieves FedRAMP Moderate Authorization for ChatGPT Enterprise and API
items.article_standfirst: OpenAI has secured FedRAMP Moderate authorization for ChatGPT Enterprise and the OpenAI API, clearing a key compliance hurdle for U.S. federal agencies deploying AI tools.
article_body: OpenAI announced on April 27, 2026, that ChatGPT Enterprise and the OpenAI API have been authorized at the FedRAMP Moderate level, a standardized security assessment used by U.S. federal agencies to evaluate cloud services handling sensitive but unclassified data.

FedRAMP Moderate is a higher bar than the more common Low authorization, requiring more rigorous controls across access management, incident response, and data isolation. Achieving it means federal agencies can now deploy OpenAI's enterprise products and API integrations within government environments under established procurement frameworks.

The authorization is particularly significant for agencies that have been limited to pilot programs or sandbox environments — it opens the door to production deployments across use cases such as document analysis, internal research tooling, and workflow automation. It also signals to state and local governments that follow federal procurement standards that a compliance pathway now exists.
article_why_matters: FedRAMP Moderate authorization is a prerequisite for many federal agencies to sign software contracts, removing a blocker that has kept AI deployments in government on hold.
ChatGPT Enterprise and the API together cover both conversational AI tools for end users and programmatic access for developers building government-integrated applications.
The milestone places OpenAI in competition with cloud AI providers such as Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud, both of which already hold FedRAMP authorizations for their respective AI services.
article_source_note: The information comes from OpenAI's own newsroom (openai.com/news/rss.xml) and the linked announcement page. The announcement is brief and does not include technical implementation details, audit findings, or which third-party assessor performed the authorization review.

items.article_h1: OpenAI Ships Alpha Release of Codex CLI, Tagging v0.126.0-alpha.8
items.article_standfirst: OpenAI has pushed a new alpha release, tagged v0.126.0-alpha.8, to its open-source Codex CLI repository, signaling continued iteration on the command-line coding agent built on the same foundation as GitHub Copilot.
article_body: OpenAI has published a new alpha release, rust-v0.126.0-alpha.8, to the public Codex repository on GitHub. The release was tagged on 2026-04-27 at 19:13:32 UTC and is visible in the repository's release feed. No release notes, changelog, or summary text were surfaced in the fetched page content.

The Codex repository hosts the official CLI tool for OpenAI's coding agent — the same model backend that powers GitHub Copilot's agentic capabilities. The "rust" prefix in the tag suggests this release relates to the Rust-based rewrite or tooling layer of the CLI, consistent with a series of alpha tags that have been landing in this feed over recent weeks.

For developers using or evaluating the Codex CLI, this tag confirms that active development is ongoing, even in the absence of detailed release notes. For teams integrating Codex into CI/CD or developer tooling pipelines, tracking alpha tags provides early signal on upcoming changes that may affect workflow behaviour or API contracts.
article_why_matters: The Codex CLI is the primary way developers directly invoke OpenAI's coding agent outside of IDE integrations, making any release notable for toolchain builders.
The Rust-prefixed alpha tag series suggests a structural rewrite or performance-focused reimplementation is under active development, not merely cosmetic updates.

Without published release notes, teams using Codex in production or pre-release evaluation cannot assess what changed — making this release a reminder that alpha-channel software requires close feed monitoring.

The release page contained a title and timestamp but no summary, changelog, or body content at the time of fetch, limiting the level of detail available. diff_excerpt: -Overview ElevenCreative ElevenAgents ElevenAPI API reference Changelog Light On this page April 20, 2026 Developer Resources ElevenAgents Speech to Text Text to Dialogue Workspaces SDK Releases API New Endpoints Deprecated Endpoints Updated Endpoints April 13, 2026 ElevenAgents Voices SDK Releases API New Endpoints Updated Endpoints April 7, 2026 ElevenAgents Speech to Text Voices SDK Releases API New Endpoints Updated Endpoints April 1, 2026 ElevenAgents Music Speech to Text Voices SDK Releases API New Endpoints Updated Endpoints March 25, 2026 Workspace March 23, 2026 ElevenAgents Music SDK Releases API New Endpoints Updated Endpoints Schema Changes March 16, 2026 ElevenAgents Workspaces Music ElevenCreative Studio SDK Releases API New Endpoints Updated Endpoints Schema Changes March 9, 2026 ElevenAgents ElevenCreative Studio Mobile Speech to Text Music Voices SDK Releases API Updated Endpoints Schema Changes March 2, 2026 ElevenAgents Pronunciation Dictionaries Music Audio Native Voices SDK Releases API New Endpoints Updated Endpoints Schema Changes February 23, 2026 ElevenAgents Workspaces Music SDK Releases API New Endpoints Removed Endpoints Updated Endpoints Changelog Subscribe via RSS April 20, 2026 April 20, 2026 Developer Resources ElevenLabs Devs YouTube channel : A new developer-focused YouTube channel is now live, featuring tutorials, API walkthroughs, and technical deep dives to help you get the most out of the ElevenLabs platform. ElevenAgents Workflow starting node override : Conversations can now be started at a specific workflow node by passing starting_workflow_node_id in the conversation initiation client data . This is gated by an opt-in enable_starting_workflow_node_id_from_client field in the agent’s platform override settings. When enabled, clients can specify which node to begin a conversation at, which is useful for agent memory and longer-term WebSocket use cases. If the specified node does not exist, the conversation falls back to the default entry point. Post-call analysis LLM configuration : Agent platform settings now include an analysis_llm field (default: gemini-2.5-flash ) that sets the default LLM for post-call analysis, including evaluation criteria and data collection. Individual data collection properties can override this default with a per-item llm field via the new AnalysisProperty schema, giving fine-grained control over which model processes each analysis item. New LLM options : Added gpt-5.4 to the list of available LLM providers for agent configuration. Added xhigh to the LLMReasoningEffort enum for extended reasoning on supported models. Null literal in workflow expressions : Workflow conditional expressions now support null comparisons via the new ASTNullNode type, enabling branches like “if variable is null” in workflow logic. Get secret by ID : A new get secret endpoint ( GET /v1/convai/secrets/{secret_id} ) retrieves a single workspace secret by its ID. The list secrets endpoint also now accepts a search query parameter to filter secrets by name prefix. Searchable phone numbers in batch calling : The batch calling form in the dashboard now includes a searchable phone number selector, making it easier to find the right outbound number when configuring batch calls. Speech to Text Speaker role detection : The convert speech to text endpoint now accepts a detect_speaker_roles parameter (boolean, default false ). When enabled alongside diarize=true , speaker labels are returned as agent and customer instead of speaker_0 , speaker_1 . This cannot be used with use_multi_channel=true . Usage incurs an additional 10% surcharge on the base transcription cost. Text to Dialogue Input character limit guidance : The text to dialogue endpoints now document a recommended limit of 2,000 characters across all inputs[].text values per request. Longer requests may terminate early in streaming responses or return a validation error. Workspaces Usage analytics endpoint : A new usage by product over time endpoint ( POST /v1/workspace/analytics/query/usage-by-product-over-time ) returns credit usage broken down by product type over time. Results are returned in a tabular structure with configurable interval_seconds , group_by dimensions, and filters . The previous GET /v1/usage/character-stats endpoint has been deprecated in favor of this new endpoint. SDK Releases Packages @elevenlabs/client@1.2.1 - Fixed conversation startup readiness so onConnect runs after the session is marked connected and React has synchronized conversationRef . Also exposed and forwarded onConversationCreated for consumers that need the created Conversation instance before onConnect . @elevenlabs/react@1.1.1 - Fixed conversation startup readiness and exposed onConversationCreated callback, matching the @elevenlabs/client@1.2.1 changes. @elevenlabs/react-native@1.1.1 , @elevenlabs/convai-widget-core@0.11.4 , @elevenlabs/convai-widget-embed@0.11.4 - Updated to @elevenlabs/client@1.2.1 with conversation startup fixes. Install it with npx skills add elevenlabs/packages You can read more about the v1 release and its improvements on our developer blog . @elevenlabs/client@1.0.0 — Breaking changes: Input and Output classes are no longer exported. Use the InputController and OutputController interfaces from @elevenlabs/client instead. Conversation is no longer a class — it is now a namespace object and a type alias for TextConversation | VoiceConversation . Remove any instanceof Conversation checks and subclasses. The default connectionType is now inferred from the conversation mode: voice conversations use "webrtc" by default, and text-only conversations use "websocket" . To keep the previous behavior for voice, pass connectionType: "websocket" explicitly. VoiceConversation.wakeLock is now private. Pass useWakeLock: false in session options to suppress wake lock management. changeInputDevice() and changeOutputDevice() now return Promise<void> instead of Promise<Input> or Promise<Output> . Replace conversation.input.analyser.getByteFrequencyData(data) with conversation.getInputByteFrequencyData() . Replace conversation.input.setMuted(v) with conversation.setMicMuted(v) . Replace conversation.output.gain.gain.value = v with conversation.setVolume({ volume: v }) . getInputVolume() , getOutputVolume() , getInputByteFrequencyData() , and getOutputByteFrequencyData() now return 0 or an empty Uint8Array instead of throwing when no conversation is active. @elevenlabs/react@1.0.0 — Breaking changes: useConversation now requires a ConversationProvider ancestor. Wrap your component tree in <ConversationProvider> and move options to the provider or to the hook. DeviceFormatConfig and DeviceInputConfig exports are removed. Use FormatConfig and InputDeviceConfig from @elevenlabs/client instead. New granular hooks replace the monolithic useConversation for better render performance: useConversationControls() , useConversationStatus() , useConversationInput() , useConversationMode() , useConversationFeedback() , and useRawConversation() . Each hook subscribes only to the state it needs, preventing unnecessary re-renders. New useConversationClientTool(name, handler) hook for registering client tools that agents can invoke, with automatic cleanup on unmount. Added controlled mute support via isMuted and onMutedChange props on ConversationProvider . @elevenlabs/react-native@1.0.0 — Breaking changes: The previous ElevenLabsProvider and useConversation API have been removed and replaced with re-exports from @elevenlabs/react . ElevenAgents Workflow starting node override : Conversations can now be started at a specific workflow node by passing starting_workflow_node_id in the conversation initiation client data . This is gated by an opt-in enable_starting_workflow_node_id_from_client field in the agent’s platform override settings. When enabled, clients can specify which node to begin a conversation at, which is useful for agent memory and longer-term WebSocket use cases. If the specified node does not exist, the conversation falls back to the default entry point. Post-call analysis LLM configuration : Agent platform settings now include an analysis_llm field (default: gemini-2.5-flash ) that sets the default LLM for post-call analysis, including evaluation criteria and data collection. Individual data collection properties can override this default with a per-item llm field via the new AnalysisProperty schema, giving fine-grained control over which model processes each analysis item. New LLM options : Added gpt-5.4 to the list of available LLM providers for agent configuration. Added xhigh to the LLMReasoningEffort enum for extended reasoning on supported models. Null literal in workflow expressions : Workflow conditional expressions now support null comparisons via the new ASTNullNode type, enabling branches like “if variable is null” in workflow logic. Get secret by ID : A new get secret endpoint ( GET /v1/convai/secrets/{secret_id} ) retrieves a single workspace secret by its ID. The list secrets endpoint also now accepts a search query parameter to filter secrets by name prefix. Searchable phone numbers in batch calling : The batch calling form in the dashboard now includes a searchable phone number selector, making it easier to find the right outbound number when configuring batch calls. Speech to Text Speaker role detection : The convert speech to text endpoint now accepts a detect_speaker_roles parameter (boolean, default false ). When enabled alongside diarize=true , speaker labels are returned as agent and customer instead of speaker_0 , speaker_1 . This cannot be used with use_multi_channel=true . Usage incurs an additional 10% surcharge on the base transcription cost. Text to Dialogue Input character limit guidance : The text to dialogue endpoints now document a recommended limit of 2,000 characters across all inputs[].text values per request. Longer requests may terminate early in streaming responses or return a validation error. Workspaces Usage analytics endpoint : A new usage by product over time endpoint ( POST /v1/workspace/analytics/query/usage-by-product-over-time ) returns credit usage broken down by product type over time. Results are returned in a tabular structure with configurable interval_seconds , group_by dimensions, and filters . The previous GET /v1/usage/character-stats endpoint has been deprecated in favor of this new endpoint. Install it with npx skills add elevenlabs/packages You can read more about the v1 release and its improvements on our developer blog . @elevenlabs/client@1.0.0 — Breaking changes: Input and Output classes are no longer exported. Use the InputController and OutputController interfaces from @elevenlabs/client instead. Conversation is no longer a class — it is now a namespace object and a type alias for TextConversation | VoiceConversation . Remove any instanceof Conversation checks and subclasses. The default connectionType is now inferred from the conversation mode: voice conversations use "webrtc" by default, and text-only conversations use "websocket" . To keep the previous behavior for voice, pass connectionType: "websocket" explicitly. VoiceConversation.wakeLock is now private. Pass useWakeLock: false in session options to suppress wake lock management. changeInputDevice() and changeOutputDevice() now return Promise<void> instead of Promise<Input> or Promise<Output> . Wrap your component tree in <ConversationProvider> and move options to the provider or to the hook. DeviceFormatConfig and DeviceInputConfig exports are removed. Use FormatConfig and InputDeviceConfig from @elevenlabs/client instead. New granular hooks replace the monolithic useConversation for better render performance: useConversationControls() , useConversationStatus() , useConversationInput() , useConversationMode() , useConversationFeedback() , and useRawConversation() . Added controlled mute support via isMuted and onMutedChange props on ConversationProvider . @elevenlabs/react-native@1.0.0 — Breaking changes: The previous ElevenLabsProvider and useConversation API have been removed and replaced with re-exports from @elevenlabs/react . Added media_generation to the type enum March 9, 2026 March 9, 2026 ElevenAgents New LLM options : Added claude-sonnet-4-6 and gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview as supported LLM providers for agent conversation configuration. Guardrail execution mode : Custom guardrails now support a configurable execution_mode field with the GuardrailExecutionMode enum, and have separate input and output schema definitions. This allows more precise control over when guardrail logic runs and which message direction it applies to. Guardrail triggered client event : Added guardrail_triggered to the ClientEvent enum, enabling agent workflows and client-side handlers to respond when a guardrail fires during a conversation. Batch calling concurrency : Added target_concurrency_limit field to the submit batch call request body, allowing you to control the target number of concurrent calls for a given batch. Agent testing type filter : The list agent tests endpoint now accepts a types query parameter to filter results by test type. The include_folders query parameter is deprecated in favor of the new types filter. Telephony call configuration : Added telephony_call_config to outbound call request schemas for Twilio and SIP trunk calls, with a default ringing_timeout_secs of 60 seconds. WhatsApp audio message responses : Added enable_audio_message_response to WhatsApp account configuration, enabling agents to send audio messages in addition to text in WhatsApp conversations. ElevenCreative Studio ElevenCreative Studio agent settings : ElevenCreative Studio project models now expose an agent_settings field using StudioAgentSettingsModel , giving agent-connected studio projects a consistent settings surface alongside the new StudioAgentToolSettingsModel . Mobile SAML SSO for iOS and Android : Enterprise customers using SAML-based identity providers can now sign in from the ElevenLabs iOS and Android apps. Speech to Text No verbatim transcription mode : Added no_verbatim as a transcription option on the convert speech to text endpoint, allowing the model to produce cleaned-up output rather than a verbatim transcript. Music SDK CRLF fix : Resolved an issue in the Python and JavaScript SDKs where the generate music detailed endpoint response was not correctly parsed when the server used \r\n\r\n line endings. The parsers now handle both \r\n and \n line endings. Voices Voice captcha font support for non-Latin scripts : Fixed an issue where the instant voice cloning captcha displayed unreadable characters for some non-Latin scripts. Added font support and correct language selection for Hebrew, Thai, Armenian, Georgian, Malayalam, Telugu, and Gurmukhi. The captcha now uses the actual language of the cloned voice rather than defaulting to English. Added rental and appointment operation types with corresponding parameter schemas March 2, 2026 March 2, 2026 ElevenAgents New widget configuration options : Several new settings are now available for the ElevenAgents conversation widget in the agent dashboard: Collapsible widget ( widget.dismissible ): Allow users to minimize or dismiss the widget during a conversation. Action indicator ( widget.show_agent_status ): Display a visual indicator when the agent is actively using tools, with distinct states for working, done, and error. Enabling this setting automatically adds agent_tool_request and agent_tool_response to the agent’s client events. Conversation ID display ( widget.show_conversation_id ): Show the conversation ID to users after a call ends. Defaults to true for both new and existing agents. Audio tag visibility ( widget.strip_audio_tags ): Hide audio tags from conversation transcripts. Defaults to true for both new and existing agents. Syntax highlighting theme ( widget.syntax_highlight_theme ): Configure code block highlighting in transcripts. Accepts null (Auto), light , or dark . Folder-aware agent testing : The List agent tests endpoint now supports organizing tests into folders. Added parent_folder_id , include_folders , and sort_mode query parameters for filtered listing. Test creation and update endpoints accept parent_folder_id to assign tests to folders, and test summaries now include entity type, parent folder path, and children count. Workflow say node : Added a new say node type to agent workflows ( WorkflowSayNodeModel ). This node supports conversation_config , additional_prompt , and tool and knowledge base overrides. The node’s message payload uses a discriminated union with two variants: literal (static text via text ) and prompt (LLM-generated via prompt ). SMB tool configuration : Formalized the SMBToolConfig schema with a required, discriminated params object. Supported operation types are create , list , search , update , and delete , each applicable to SMB entity categories including clients, staff, services, products, and assets. Summary language for agents : Added summary_language field to agent configuration to specify the language for post-conversation summaries. Twilio call recording : Added optional call_recording_enabled field to the Twilio outbound call request body to enable recording of outbound calls. WhatsApp messaging flag : Added enable_messaging flag to WhatsApp account models and update requests to control whether messaging is enabled for a given phone number. Pronunciation Dictionaries Set all rules at once : New Set rules endpoint ( POST /v1/pronunciation-dictionaries/{pronunciation_dictionary_id}/set-rules ) replaces all existing rules in a pronunciation dictionary in a single call, as an alternative to incrementally adding or removing individual rules. Rule matching options : Added case_sensitive (boolean) and word_boundaries (boolean) fields to pronunciation dictionary rule definitions for more precise control over how rules match text. Music Upload audio : New Upload music endpoint ( POST /v1/music/upload ) accepts a multipart/form-data request with an audio file. Optionally extracts a composition plan from the uploaded audio and returns a MusicUploadResponse with a song_id . Phonetic name support : Added use_phonetic_names parameter to Generate music , Generate music detailed , and Stream music endpoints. Song ID in responses : Music generation responses now surface the song_id field, making it easier to reference generated songs in subsequent API calls. Audio Native Update project content from URL : New Update content endpoint ( POST /v1/audio-native/content ) allows updating an AudioNative project by providing a URL. The endpoint extracts the content from the URL and queues it for conversion and auto-publishing. Voices Voice bookmarking : Added bookmarked field to voice update requests and is_bookmarked to voice response objects, enabling users to bookmark voices for easy access. ElevenAgents Workflow starting node override : Conversations can now be started at a specific workflow node by passing starting_workflow_node_id in the conversation initiation client data . This is gated by an opt-in enable_starting_workflow_node_id_from_client field in the agent’s platform override settings. When enabled, clients can specify which node to begin a conversation at, which is useful for agent memory and longer-term WebSocket use cases. If the specified node does not exist, the conversation falls back to the default entry point. Post-call analysis LLM configuration : Agent platform settings now include an analysis_llm field (default: gemini-2.5-flash ) that sets the default LLM for post-call analysis, including evaluation criteria and data collection. Individual data collection properties can override this default with a per-item llm field via the new AnalysisProperty schema, giving fine-grained control over which model processes each analysis item. New LLM options : Added gpt-5.4 to the list of available LLM providers for agent configuration. Added xhigh to the LLMReasoningEffort enum for extended reasoning on supported models. Null literal in workflow expressions : Workflow conditional expressions now support null comparisons via the new ASTNullNode type, enabling branches like “if variable is null” in workflow logic. Get secret by ID : A new get secret endpoint ( GET /v1/convai/secrets/{secret_id} ) retrieves a single workspace secret by its ID. The list secrets endpoint also now accepts a search query parameter to filter secrets by name prefix. Searchable phone numbers in batch calling : The batch calling form in the dashboard now includes a searchable phone number selector, making it easier to find the right outbound number when configuring batch calls. Speech to Text Speaker role detection : The convert speech to text endpoint now accepts a detect_speaker_roles parameter (boolean, default false ). When enabled alongside diarize=true , speaker labels are returned as agent and customer instead of speaker_0 , speaker_1 . This cannot be used with use_multi_channel=true . Usage incurs an additional 10% surcharge on the base transcription cost. Text to Dialogue Input character limit guidance : The text to dialogue endpoints now document a recommended limit of 2,000 characters across all inputs[].text values per request. Longer requests may terminate early in streaming responses or return a validation error. Workspaces Usage analytics endpoint : A new usage by product over time endpoint ( POST /v1/workspace/analytics/query/usage-by-product-over-time ) returns credit usage broken down by product type over time. Results are returned in a tabular structure with configurable interval_seconds , group_by dimensions, and filters . The previous GET /v1/usage/character-stats endpoint has been deprecated in favor of this new endpoint. Secret dependency management : A new get secret dependencies endpoint ( GET /v1/convai/secrets/{secret_id}/dependencies/{resource_type} ) returns paginated dependency lookups filtered by resource type ( tools , agents , or phone_numbers ). Voices Non-community voice filter : The voice_type query parameter on the list voices endpoint now accepts non-community , which returns personal and workspace voices combined while excluding library copies. This provides a convenient filter for teams that want to see only their own voices without community-shared ones. It features major breaking changes to @elevenlabs/client , @elevenlabs/react , and @elevenlabs/react-native . Review the migration guidance below before upgrading. To help upgrade, we released a Skill to help your agents upgrade for you. Install it with npx skills add elevenlabs/packages You can read more about the v1 release and its improvements on our developer blog . @elevenlabs/client@1.0.0 — Breaking changes: Input and Output classes are no longer exported. Use the InputController and OutputController interfaces from @elevenlabs/client instead. Conversation is no longer a class — it is now a namespace object and a type alias for TextConversation | VoiceConversation . Remove any instanceof Conversation checks and subclasses. The default connectionType is now inferred from the conversation mode: voice conversations use "webrtc" by default, and text-only conversations use "websocket" . To keep the previous behavior for voice, pass connectionType: "websocket" explicitly. VoiceConversation.wakeLock is now private. Pass useWakeLock: false in session options to suppress wake lock management. changeInputDevice() and changeOutputDevice() now return Promise<void> instead of Promise<Input> or Promise<Output> . Replace conversation.input.analyser.getByteFrequencyData(data) with conversation.getInputByteFrequencyData() . Replace conversation.input.setMuted(v) with conversation.setMicMuted(v) . Replace conversation.output.gain.gain.value = v with conversation.setVolume({ volume: v }) . getInputVolume() , getOutputVolume() , getInputByteFrequencyData() , and getOutputByteFrequencyData() now return 0 or an empty Uint8Array instead of throwing when no conversation is active. @elevenlabs/react@1.0.0 — Breaking changes: useConversation now requires a ConversationProvider ancestor. Wrap your component tree in <ConversationProvider> and move options to the provider or to the hook. DeviceFormatConfig and DeviceInputConfig exports are removed. Use FormatConfig and InputDeviceConfig from @elevenlabs/client instead. New granular hooks replace the monolithic useConversation for better render performance: useConversationControls() , useConversationStatus() , useConversationInput() , useConversationMode() , useConversationFeedback() , and useRawConversation() . Each hook subscribes only to the state it needs, preventing unnecessary re-renders. New useConversationClientTool(name, handler) hook for registering client tools that agents can invoke, with automatic cleanup on unmount. Added controlled mute support via isMuted and onMutedChange props on ConversationProvider . @elevenlabs/react-native@1.0.0 — Breaking changes: The previous ElevenLabsProvider and useConversation API have been removed and replaced with re-exports from @elevenlabs/react . Replace ElevenLabsProvider with ConversationProvider and useConversation with the granular hooks ( useConversationControls , useConversationStatus , etc.). This is a major release with breaking changes that improve the API surface, add granular React hooks for better render performance, and unify the React Native SDK with the React SDK. To try out the release candidate: $ npm install @elevenlabs/client@next @elevenlabs/react@next @elevenlabs/react-native@next An elevenlabs:sdk-migration skill is available to help AI coding assistants automatically migrate your codebase to the new APIs. Add it to your Claude Code, Cursor, or Windsurf project to get guided migration support. New granular hooks for fine-grained re-rendering: useConversationControls() , useConversationStatus() , useConversationInput() , useConversationMode() , useConversationFeedback() . New useConversationClientTool() hook for dynamically registering client tools from React components with full type safety. @elevenlabs/react-native : Complete API rewrite replacing ElevenLabsProvider and useConversation with ConversationProvider and the same granular hooks from @elevenlabs/react . WebRTC polyfills and native AudioSession configuration are applied automatically on import. Added media_generation to the type enum March 9, 2026 March 9, 2026 ElevenAgents New LLM options : Added claude-sonnet-4-6 and gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview as supported LLM providers for agent conversation configuration. Guardrail execution mode : Custom guardrails now support a configurable execution_mode field with the GuardrailExecutionMode enum, and have separate input and output schema definitions. This allows more precise control over when guardrail logic runs and which message direction it applies to. Guardrail triggered client event : Added guardrail_triggered to the ClientEvent enum, enabling agent workflows and client-side handlers to respond when a guardrail fires during a conversation. Batch calling concurrency : Added target_concurrency_limit field to the submit batch call request body, allowing you to control the target number of concurrent calls for a given batch. Agent testing type filter : The list agent tests endpoint now accepts a types query parameter to filter results by test type. The include_folders query parameter is deprecated in favor of the new types filter. Telephony call configuration : Added telephony_call_config to outbound call request schemas for Twilio and SIP trunk calls, with a default ringing_timeout_secs of 60 seconds. WhatsApp audio message responses : Added enable_audio_message_response to WhatsApp account configuration, enabling agents to send audio messages in addition to text in WhatsApp conversations. ElevenCreative Studio ElevenCreative Studio agent settings : ElevenCreative Studio project models now expose an agent_settings field using StudioAgentSettingsModel , giving agent-connected studio projects a consistent settings surface alongside the new StudioAgentToolSettingsModel . Mobile SAML SSO for iOS and Android : Enterprise customers using SAML-based identity providers can now sign in from the ElevenLabs iOS and Android apps. Speech to Text No verbatim transcription mode : Added no_verbatim as a transcription option on the convert speech to text endpoint, allowing the model to produce cleaned-up output rather than a verbatim transcript. Music SDK CRLF fix : Resolved an issue in the Python and JavaScript SDKs where the generate music detailed endpoint response was not correctly parsed when the server used \r\n\r\n line endings. The parsers now handle both \r\n and \n line endings. Voices Voice captcha font support for non-Latin scripts : Fixed an issue where the instant voice cloning captcha displayed unreadable characters for some non-Latin scripts. Added font support and correct language selection for Hebrew, Thai, Armenian, Georgian, Malayalam, Telugu, and Gurmukhi. The captcha now uses the actual language of the cloned voice rather than defaulting to English. Added rental and appointment operation types with corresponding parameter schemas March 2, 2026 March 2, 2026 ElevenAgents New widget configuration options : Several new settings are now available for the ElevenAgents conversation widget in the agent dashboard: Collapsible widget ( widget.dismissible ): Allow users to minimize or dismiss the widget during a conversation. Action indicator ( widget.show_agent_status ): Display a visual indicator when the agent is actively using tools, with distinct states for working, done, and error. Enabling this setting automatically adds agent_tool_request and agent_tool_response to the agent’s client events. Conversation ID display ( widget.show_conversation_id ): Show the conversation ID to users after a call ends. Defaults to true for both new and existing agents. Audio tag visibility ( widget.strip_audio_tags ): Hide audio tags from conversation transcripts. Defaults to true for both new and existing agents. Syntax highlighting theme ( widget.syntax_highlight_theme ): Configure code block highlighting in transcripts. Accepts null (Auto), light , or dark . Folder-aware agent testing : The List agent tests endpoint now supports organizing tests into folders. Added parent_folder_id , include_folders , and sort_mode query parameters for filtered listing. Test creation and update endpoints accept parent_folder_id to assign tests to folders, and test summaries now include entity type, parent folder path, and children count. Workflow say node : Added a new say node type to agent workflows ( WorkflowSayNodeModel ). This node supports conversation_config , additional_prompt , and tool and knowledge base overrides. The node’s message payload uses a discriminated union with two variants: literal (static text via text ) and prompt (LLM-generated via prompt ). SMB tool configuration : Formalized the SMBToolConfig schema with a required, discriminated params object. Supported operation types are create , list , search , update , and delete , each applicable to SMB entity categories including clients, staff, services, products, and assets. Summary language for agents : Added summary_language field to agent configuration to specify the language for post-conversation summaries. Twilio call recording : Added optional call_recording_enabled field to the Twilio outbound call request body to enable recording of outbound calls. WhatsApp messaging flag : Added enable_messaging flag to WhatsApp account models and update requests to control whether messaging is enabled for a given phone number. Pronunciation Dictionaries Set all rules at once : New Set rules endpoint ( POST /v1/pronunciation-dictionaries/{pronunciation_dictionary_id}/set-rules ) replaces all existing rules in a pronunciation dictionary in a single call, as an alternative to incrementally adding or removing individual rules. Rule matching options : Added case_sensitive (boolean) and word_boundaries (boolean) fields to pronunciation dictionary rule definitions for more precise control over how rules match text. Music Upload audio : New Upload music endpoint ( POST /v1/music/upload ) accepts a multipart/form-data request with an audio file. Optionally extracts a composition plan from the uploaded audio and returns a MusicUploadResponse with a song_id . Phonetic name support : Added use_phonetic_names parameter to Generate music , Generate music detailed , and Stream music endpoints. Song ID in responses : Music generation responses now surface the song_id field, making it easier to reference generated songs in subsequent API calls. Audio Native Update project content from URL : New Update content endpoint ( POST /v1/audio-native/content ) allows updating an AudioNative project by providing a URL. The endpoint extracts the content from the URL and queues it for conversion and auto-publishing. Voices Voice bookmarking : Added bookmarked field to voice update requests and is_bookmarked to voice response objects, enabling users to bookmark voices for easy access. ElevenAgents Workflow starting node override : Conversations can now be started at a specific workflow node by passing starting_workflow_node_id in the conversation initiation client data . This is gated by an opt-in enable_starting_workflow_node_id_from_client field in the agent’s platform override settings. When enabled, clients can specify which node to begin a conversation at, which is useful for agent memory and longer-term WebSocket use cases. If the specified node does not exist, the conversation falls back to the default entry point. Post-call analysis LLM configuration : Agent platform settings now include an analysis_llm field (default: gemini-2.5-flash ) that sets the default LLM for post-call analysis, including evaluation criteria and data collection. Individual data collection properties can override this default with a per-item llm field via the new AnalysisProperty schema, giving fine-grained control over which model processes each analysis item. New LLM options : Added gpt-5.4 to the list of available LLM providers for agent configuration. Added xhigh to the LLMReasoningEffort enum for extended reasoning on supported models. Null literal in workflow expressions : Workflow conditional expressions now support null comparisons via the new ASTNullNode type, enabling branches like “if variable is null” in workflow logic. Get secret by ID : A new get secret endpoint ( GET /v1/convai/secrets/{secret_id} ) retrieves a single workspace secret by its ID. The list secrets endpoint also now accepts a search query parameter to filter secrets by name prefix. Searchable phone numbers in batch calling : The batch calling form in the dashboard now includes a searchable phone number selector, making it easier to find the right outbound number when configuring batch calls. Speech to Text Speaker role detection : The convert speech to text endpoint now accepts a detect_speaker_roles parameter (boolean, default false ). When enabled alongside diarize=true , speaker labels are returned as agent and customer instead of speaker_0 , speaker_1 . This cannot be used with use_multi_channel=true . Usage incurs an additional 10% surcharge on the base transcription cost. Text to Dialogue Input character limit guidance : The text to dialogue endpoints now document a recommended limit of 2,000 characters across all inputs[].text values per request. Longer requests may terminate early in streaming responses or return a validation error. Workspaces Usage analytics endpoint : A new usage by product over time endpoint ( POST /v1/workspace/analytics/query/usage-by-product-over-time ) returns credit usage broken down by product type over time. Results are returned in a tabular structure with configurable interval_seconds , group_by dimensions, and filters . The previous GET /v1/usage/character-stats endpoint has been deprecated in favor of this new endpoint. It features major breaking changes to @elevenlabs/client , @elevenlabs/react , and @elevenlabs/react-native . Review the migration guidance below before upgrading. To help upgrade, we released a Skill to help your agents upgrade for you. Install it with npx skills add elevenlabs/packages You can read more about the v1 release and its improvements on our developer blog . @elevenlabs/client@1.0.0 — Breaking changes: Input and Output classes are no longer exported. Use the InputController and OutputController interfaces from @elevenlabs/client instead. Conversation is no longer a class — it is now a namespace object and a type alias for TextConversation | VoiceConversation . Remove any instanceof Conversation checks and subclasses. The default connectionType is now inferred from the conversation mode: voice conversations use "webrtc" by default, and text-only conversations use "websocket" . To keep the previous behavior for voice, pass connectionType: "websocket" explicitly. VoiceConversation.wakeLock is now private. Pass useWakeLock: false in session options to suppress wake lock management. changeInputDevice() and changeOutputDevice() now return Promise<void> instead of Promise<Input> or Promise<Output> . Replace conversation.input.analyser.getByteFrequencyData(data) with conversation.getInputByteFrequencyData() . Replace conversation.input.setMuted(v) with conversation.setMicMuted(v) . Replace conversation.output.gain.gain.value = v with conversation.setVolume({ volume: v }) . getInputVolume() , getOutputVolume() , getInputByteFrequencyData() , and getOutputByteFrequencyData() now return 0 or an empty Uint8Array instead of throwing when no conversation is active. @elevenlabs/react@1.0.0 — Breaking changes: useConversation now requires a ConversationProvider ancestor. Wrap your component tree in <ConversationProvider> and move options to the provider or to the hook. DeviceFormatConfig and DeviceInputConfig exports are removed. Use FormatConfig and InputDeviceConfig from @elevenlabs/client instead. New granular hooks replace the monolithic useConversation for better render performance: useConversationControls() , useConversationStatus() , useConversationInput() , useConversationMode() , useConversationFeedback() , and useRawConversation() . Each hook subscribes only to the state it needs, preventing unnecessary re-renders. New useConversationClientTool(name, handler) hook for registering client tools that agents can invoke, with automatic cleanup on unmount. Added controlled mute support via isMuted and onMutedChange props on ConversationProvider . @elevenlabs/react-native@1.0.0 — Breaking changes: The previous ElevenLabsProvider and useConversation API have been removed and replaced with re-exports from @elevenlabs/react . Replace ElevenLabsProvider with ConversationProvider and useConversation with the granular hooks ( useConversationControls , useConversationStatus , etc.). This is a major release with breaking changes that improve the API surface, add granular React hooks for better render performance, and unify the React Native SDK with the React SDK. To try out the release candidate: $ npm install @elevenlabs/client@next @elevenlabs/react@next @elevenlabs/react-native@next An elevenlabs:sdk-migration skill is available to help AI coding assistants automatically migrate your codebase to the new APIs. Add it to your Claude Code, Cursor, or Windsurf project to get guided migration support. New granular hooks for fine-grained re-rendering: useConversationControls() , useConversationStatus() , useConversationInput() , useConversationMode() , useConversationFeedback() . New useConversationClientTool() hook for dynamically registering client tools from React components with full type safety. @elevenlabs/react-native : Complete API rewrite replacing ElevenLabsProvider and useConversation with ConversationProvider and the same granular hooks from @elevenlabs/react . WebRTC polyfills and native AudioSession configuration are applied automatically on import. Updated Endpoints ElevenAgents List conversations Added tool_names_successful query parameter (array of strings, optional) to filter conversations where specified tools had successful calls Added tool_names_errored query parameter (array of strings, optional) to filter conversations where specified tools returned errors Text search conversations Added tool_names_successful query parameter (array of strings, optional) Added tool_names_errored query parameter (array of strings, optional) List users Added sort_by query parameter (string, optional) accepting last_contact_unix_secs (default) or conversation_count Added branch_id query parameter (string, optional) to filter users by agent branch Delete tool Added force query parameter (boolean, optional, default false ) to delete a tool regardless of agent dependencies, automatically removing it from all dependent agents and branches Get ElevenAgents settings , Update ElevenAgents settings Added conversation_embedding_retention_days field (integer, optional, max 365) to configure how long conversation embeddings are retained Workspaces Invite multiple users Added seat_type field (string, optional) to specify the seat type for all invited users Music Generate music detailed Added respect_sections_durations field (boolean, optional) to control whether the model adheres to per-section durations in the composition plan ElevenCreative Studio Get chapter , Add chapter , Update chapter Added has_visual_content field (boolean) to chapter response models Schema Changes New Schemas ContentThresholdGuardrail - Threshold-based content guardrail with is_enabled (boolean) and threshold (number, default 0.3 ) fields AgentPromptChangeToolConfig - System tool configuration for dynamic agent prompt changes StudioTextStyleShadowModel - Text shadow styling for ElevenCreative Studio StudioTextStyleOutlineModel - Text outline styling for ElevenCreative Studio UsersSortBy - Enum for user listing sort options: last_contact_unix_secs , conversation_count VideoAnalysis , VideoAnalysisResult , VideoKeyMoment , VideoSegment , VideoSubject , VideoTranscription , VideoTranscriptionWord - Video analysis result schemas for clip processing Modified Schemas ConversationHistorySIPTrunkingPhoneCallModel - Added sip_header_dynamic_variables field for inbound SIP custom X-headers PendingClipTask - Added media_generation to the type enum March 9, 2026 March 9, 2026 ElevenAgents New LLM options : Added claude-sonnet-4-6 and gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview as supported LLM providers for agent conversation configuration. Guardrail execution mode : Custom guardrails now support a configurable execution_mode field with the GuardrailExecutionMode enum, and have separate input and output schema definitions. This allows more precise control over when guardrail logic runs and which message direction it applies to. Guardrail triggered client event : Added guardrail_triggered to the ClientEvent enum, enabling agent workflows and client-side handlers to respond when a guardrail fires during a conversation. Batch calling concurrency : Added target_concurrency_limit field to the submit batch call request body, allowing you to control the target number of concurrent calls for a given batch. Agent testing type filter : The list agent tests endpoint now accepts a types query parameter to filter results by test type. The include_folders query parameter is deprecated in favor of the new types filter. Telephony call configuration : Added telephony_call_config to outbound call request schemas for Twilio and SIP trunk calls, with a default ringing_timeout_secs of 60 seconds. WhatsApp audio message responses : Added enable_audio_message_response to WhatsApp account configuration, enabling agents to send audio messages in addition to text in WhatsApp conversations. ElevenCreative Studio ElevenCreative Studio agent settings : ElevenCreative Studio project models now expose an agent_settings field using StudioAgentSettingsModel , giving agent-connected studio projects a consistent settings surface alongside the new StudioAgentToolSettingsModel . Mobile SAML SSO for iOS and Android : Enterprise customers using SAML-based identity providers can now sign in from the ElevenLabs iOS and Android apps. Speech to Text No verbatim transcription mode : Added no_verbatim as a transcription option on the convert speech to text endpoint, allowing the model to produce cleaned-up output rather than a verbatim transcript. Music SDK CRLF fix : Resolved an issue in the Python and JavaScript SDKs where the generate music detailed endpoint response was not correctly parsed when the server used \r\n\r\n line endings. The parsers now handle both \r\n and \n line endings. Voices Voice captcha font support for non-Latin scripts : Fixed an issue where the instant voice cloning captcha displayed unreadable characters for some non-Latin scripts. Added font support and correct language selection for Hebrew, Thai, Armenian, Georgian, Malayalam, Telugu, and Gurmukhi. The captcha now uses the actual language of the cloned voice rather than defaulting to English. SDK Releases JavaScript SDK v2.38.0 - Fern regeneration to match the latest API schema, including new ElevenAgents fields, updated SMB tool types, and studio agent settings. v2.38.1 - Fixed CRLF parsing for the music detailed endpoint response stream. Python SDK v2.38.0 - Fern regeneration to match the latest API schema, including new ElevenAgents fields, updated SMB tool types, and studio agent settings. v2.38.1 - Fixed CRLF parsing for the music detailed endpoint response stream. Packages @elevenlabs/convai-widget-core@0.10.1 - Fixed a widget crash on Wix sites where Wix security hardening makes addEventListener non-writable. @elevenlabs/convai-widget-core@0.10.2 - Registered missing livekit-client pnpm patch in patchedDependencies . @elevenlabs/convai-widget-embed@0.10.1 - Fixed a widget crash on Wix sites where addEventListener is non-writable. @elevenlabs/convai-widget-embed@0.10.2 - Registered missing livekit-client pnpm patch in patchedDependencies . API View API changes Updated Endpoints ElevenAgents Create agent Added enable_versioning query parameter (boolean, optional) to enable version tracking on creation Update agent Added enable_versioning_if_not_enabled query parameter (boolean, optional) to opt existing agents into versioning List agent tests Added types query parameter (string, optional) to filter tests by type Deprecated include_folders query parameter Submit batch call Added target_concurrency_limit field (integer, optional) to set the target number of concurrent calls Twilio outbound call , SIP trunk outbound call Added telephony_call_config field with TelephonyCallConfig schema (default ringing_timeout_secs: 60 ) Get conversation Added environment field to conversation records Get WhatsApp account , Update WhatsApp account Added enable_audio_message_response field (boolean, optional) Speech to Text Convert speech to text Added no_verbatim transcription option Removed scribe_v2_turbo from ASRProvider enum Audio Native Update Audio Native content Made author and title fields optional in update requests ElevenCreative Studio Get project , Add project , Update project Added agent_settings field using StudioAgentSettingsModel Clip responses now include playback_speed field Schema Changes New Schemas TelephonyCallConfig - Configuration for telephony outbound calls including ringing_timeout_secs GuardrailExecutionMode - Enum controlling when custom guardrail logic executes StudioAgentSettingsModel - Agent settings attached to studio projects StudioAgentToolSettingsModel - Tool-level settings within studio agent configuration CheckServiceAvailabilityParams , CreateAssetParams , CreateClientAppointmentParams - Additional SMB tool discriminated parameter types for rental and appointment workflows Modified Schemas ClientEvent - Added guardrail_triggered enum value CustomGuardrailConfig - Added execution_mode field (string, optional) and separate input/output schema definitions ConversationUserResponseModel - last_contact_agent_id is now nullable and non-required SMBToolConfig - Added rental and appointment operation types with corresponding parameter schemas March 2, 2026 March 2, 2026 ElevenAgents New widget configuration options : Several new settings are now available for the ElevenAgents conversation widget in the agent dashboard: Collapsible widget ( widget.dismissible ): Allow users to minimize or dismiss the widget during a conversation. Action indicator ( widget.show_agent_status ): Display a visual indicator when the agent is actively using tools, with distinct states for working, done, and error. Enabling this setting automatically adds agent_tool_request and agent_tool_response to the agent’s client events. Conversation ID display ( widget.show_conversation_id ): Show the conversation ID to users after a call ends. Defaults to true for both new and existing agents. Audio tag visibility ( widget.strip_audio_tags ): Hide audio tags from conversation transcripts. Defaults to true for both new and existing agents. Syntax highlighting theme ( widget.syntax_highlight_theme ): Configure code block highlighting in transcripts. Accepts null (Auto), light , or dark . Folder-aware agent testing : The List agent tests endpoint now supports organizing tests into folders. Added parent_folder_id , include_folders , and sort_mode query parameters for filtered listing. Test creation and update endpoints accept parent_folder_id to assign tests to folders, and test summaries now include entity type, parent folder path, and children count. Workflow say node : Added a new say node type to agent workflows ( WorkflowSayNodeModel ). This node supports conversation_config , additional_prompt , and tool and knowledge base overrides. The node’s message payload uses a discriminated union with two variants: literal (static text via text ) and prompt (LLM-generated via prompt ). SMB tool configuration : Formalized the SMBToolConfig schema with a required, discriminated params object. Supported operation types are create , list , search , update , and delete , each applicable to SMB entity categories including clients, staff, services, products, and assets. Summary language for agents : Added summary_language field to agent configuration to specify the language for post-conversation summaries. Twilio call recording : Added optional call_recording_enabled field to the Twilio outbound call request body to enable recording of outbound calls. WhatsApp messaging flag : Added enable_messaging flag to WhatsApp account models and update requests to control whether messaging is enabled for a given phone number. Pronunciation Dictionaries Set all rules at once : New Set rules endpoint ( POST /v1/pronunciation-dictionaries/{pronunciation_dictionary_id}/set-rules ) replaces all existing rules in a pronunciation dictionary in a single call, as an alternative to incrementally adding or removing individual rules. Rule matching options : Added case_sensitive (boolean) and word_boundaries (boolean) fields to pronunciation dictionary rule definitions for more precise control over how rules match text. Music Upload audio : New Upload music endpoint ( POST /v1/music/upload ) accepts a multipart/form-data request with an audio file. Optionally extracts a composition plan from the uploaded audio and returns a MusicUploadResponse with a song_id . Phonetic name support : Added use_phonetic_names parameter to Generate music , Generate music detailed , and Stream music endpoints. Song ID in responses : Music generation responses now surface the song_id field, making it easier to reference generated songs in subsequent API calls. Audio Native Update project content from URL : New Update content endpoint ( POST /v1/audio-native/content ) allows updating an AudioNative project by providing a URL. The endpoint extracts the content from the URL and queues it for conversion and auto-publishing. Voices Voice bookmarking : Added bookmarked field to voice update requests and is_bookmarked to voice response objects, enabling users to bookmark voices for easy access. Workspaces Image and video generation permission : Workspace administrators can now control access to image and video generation features. items.article_h1: ElevenLabs Updates API, Agents, and Creative Tools Across Multiple Weeks in April 2026
items.article_standfirst: ElevenLabs has shipped a series of API, SDK, and product updates across its voice and audio platform over the past three weeks, adding new endpoints, updating existing ones, and expanding its developer tooling around speech-to-text, voice synthesis, and AI agents.
article_body: The ElevenLabs changelog for April 2026 shows a sustained cadence of updates spanning three weekly release windows — April 20, April 13, and April 7 — touching multiple platform pillars including the ElevenAgents suite, the ElevenAPI reference, ElevenCreative Studio, the Workspaces feature, and the underlying SDK and API layers. Changes logged include new endpoints, updated endpoints, deprecated endpoints, and schema changes, though specific endpoint names and change details are not enumerated in the visible changelog page.

The platform's core capabilities under active development include Speech to Text, Text to Dialogue, and Music generation, alongside new or revised tooling in the ElevenCreative Studio. The API layer — which covers both new and updated endpoints — and the SDK continue to receive structured releases, suggesting ongoing investment in the developer-facing interface. Workspaces, the collaborative environment for managing voices and projects, also appears in this period's updates.

For developers building voice-enabled applications, the pattern of weekly API and SDK releases signals a platform still in active iteration, with new endpoints being added faster than existing ones are being deprecated. The co-occurrence of API changes with ElevenAgents — ElevenLabs' conversational AI agent product — suggests tighter integration between voice synthesis and agent runtimes, which matters for teams building real-time voice interfaces.
article_why_matters: Weekly API and SDK releases indicate ElevenLabs is actively expanding its surface area for developers, not just maintaining it.
The simultaneous update of ElevenAgents alongside the core API suggests voice synthesis is being woven more deeply into agentic application stacks.
Schema changes in this period could require app developers to audit their data models, as breaking changes to response shapes are plausible with that label.
article_source_note: This brief is drawn from the public ElevenLabs changelog page (elevenlabs.io/changelog), which lists weekly update dates and the categories affected — including API, SDK, ElevenAgents, ElevenCreative, Workspaces, and Music — but does not expose the specific new or updated endpoint names, parameter changes, or feature descriptions in the rendered excerpt. The full detail is presumably behind the docs site linked from the changelog. The information is sufficient to establish update cadence and affected products, but not detailed enough to report on concrete API contracts or feature specifics.

items.article_h1: Accenture Begins Deploying Microsoft 365 Copilot to a Workforce the Size of Denver
items.article_standfirst: Microsoft and Accenture are in the early phases of rolling out Microsoft 365 Copilot to Accenture's roughly 750,000 employees globally, with the partnership outlining a structured approach to managing the deployment at scale.
article_body: Microsoft has confirmed that Accenture is deploying Microsoft 365 Copilot across its global workforce of approximately 750,000 people, a figure comparable to the population of Denver, Colorado. A dedicated post on the Microsoft AI Blog details how the two companies are approaching the rollout.

Microsoft describes the partnership as entering what it calls 'the next phase,' with Accenture acting as a reference customer for large-scale enterprise Copilot deployment. The Microsoft AI Blog post is titled 'Here\'s how they\'re doing it,' suggesting a structured methodology for managing the rollout across a workforce that spans multiple countries, industries, and internal teams.

The practical significance for other enterprises is straightforward: Accenture\'s scale makes it a real-world stress test for Microsoft 365 Copilot governance, licensing, data governance, and user onboarding at an unusually large scope. Teams evaluating or already mid-rollout with Copilot will be watching for lessons on how such a deployment is managed, measured, and secured across a global workforce.
article_why_matters: At 750,000 seats, Accenture\'s deployment is among the largest known Microsoft 365 Copilot rollouts publicly confirmed, making it a benchmark for enterprise-scale adoption.
The partnership gives Microsoft a reference customer to validate its AI productivity tools in a complex, multinational professional services environment.
For organizations already mid-rollout or evaluating Copilot, Accenture\'s approach to governance, security, and user enablement will surface practical lessons they can apply to their own deployments.
article_source_note: The source is the Microsoft AI Blog (blogs.microsoft.com/ai/), which lists Accenture as a customer in a tag-indexed case studies section. The blog teases a detailed post titled 'Here\'s how they\'re doing it,' but the full article body was not returned in the fetch — the source text field is empty. Details about the deployment methodology, timeline, or scope beyond what appears in the tag listing are not available in the current evidence.

items.article_h1: Stability AI Launches Brand Studio, an End-to-End Creative Production Platform
items.article_standfirst: Stability AI has introduced Brand Studio, a new platform that anchors AI-assisted creative production to a brand's own visual identity, signaling the company's expansion beyond foundation models into integrated marketing workflows.
article_body: Stability AI announced Brand Studio on April 8, 2026, describing it as an 'end-to-end creative production platform powered by your brand.' The product appears designed to let organizations generate on-brand imagery, copy, or campaign assets at scale rather than working from generic model outputs.

The announcement is brief: no model specifications, feature list, API availability, or pricing tiers are included in the page capture. Stability AI's news page lists two other recent initiatives — joining the Tech Coalition (a child-safety alliance) in February and a Warner Music Group partnership in November 2025 — but the Brand Studio launch is the sole new product announcement on the current page.

Brand Studio positions Stability AI to compete more directly with platforms targeting enterprise marketing and creative teams, a crowded space where Midjourney, Adobe Firefly, and Canva all offer brand-consistent AI tools. Without a detailed feature breakdown, prospective users will need to request access or a demo to assess how the platform actually enforces brand alignment in practice.
article_why_matters: Brand Studio marks Stability AI's shift from offering model access to offering a complete creative workflow, blurring the line between AI vendor and marketing-tech platform.
The 'powered by your brand' framing suggests customization baked in — a key differentiator when generic AI output remains a common enterprise complaint.

The thin public announcement (no feature specs, no API details, no availability date) means the platform's real capabilities and limitations remain undisclosed until Stability AI shares more or allows hands-on evaluation.
article_source_note: This brief is drawn from the public Stability AI news page (stability.ai/news), which provided only a short teaser description of Brand Studio — no detailed feature list, model specs, or usage terms. The Warner Music Group partnership and Tech Coalition membership offer context but are separate initiatives. A full product review or published demo would be needed to assess Brand Studio's actual capabilities.

items.article_h1: llama.cpp Lands Fast Mat-Vec Kernels for Inline Quantization in Release b8951
items.article_standfirst: The ggml-org/llama.cpp project tagged release b8951 on April 27, centering on optimized matrix-vector kernels for inline quantized weights, with pre-built binaries now covering a wider cross-section of desktop, mobile, server, and Chinese OS platforms.
article_body: The llama.cpp project at ggml-org issued release tag b8951 on April 27, 2026, the same day the monitored release feed refreshed at 17:41:34 UTC. The headline change is the addition of fast mat-vec (matrix-vector multiplication) kernels for inline quantized weights, merged under pull request #22344. Alongside the core optimization, the release ships pre-built binaries for an expanded list of platforms and hardware backends.

The binaries now cover macOS Apple Silicon with and without KleidiAI acceleration, macOS Intel (x64), iOS via XCFramework, and a broad set of Linux targets spanning Ubuntu on x64 and arm64 CPUs, plus Vulkan, ROCm 7.2, OpenVINO, and SYCL (both FP32 and FP16) builds. Windows builds are available for CPU, CUDA 12 and 13, Vulkan, SYCL, and HIP backends. Notably, openEuler builds for both x86 and aarch64 architectures — covering Huawei Ascend 310P and 910B with ACL Graph — signal continued investment in Chinese enterprise and AI accelerator ecosystems.

For inference engineers and developers running quantized LLMs on custom or resource-constrained hardware, the mat-vec kernel improvements directly affect throughput and latency. The breadth of platform coverage — from iOS to openEuler Ascend targets — suggests llama.cpp is being used not only for local desktop inference but increasingly as a portable inference runtime for mobile and enterprise AI deployments where CUDA is not available.
article_why_matters: Fast inline quantized mat-vec kernels are among the most impactful optimizations for running large language models on CPU or mixed hardware, directly reducing inference latency without model surgery.

Expanded platform coverage — particularly iOS, openEuler with Ascend 310P/910B support — shows llama.cpp filling gaps left by proprietary inference runtimes, making quantized model serving viable across Apple Silicon, Huawei AI accelerators, and Linux enterprise servers.

The continued rapid release cadence (tagged one to several times daily) and broad hardware backend matrix make llama.cpp a living baseline for open-weight inference research and product deployments alike.
article_source_note: Information is drawn from the public GitHub release page for ggml-org/llama.cpp tag b8951, published at 2026-04-27T17:41:34Z, and the release's linked evidence excerpt covering the PR title, platform binaries, and hardware backends. The source does not include a full changelog or release notes text, so the depth of the technical change is inferred from the PR title and binary matrix rather than detailed commit history.

items.article_h1: Hugging Face Gradio Spaces Now Auto-Generate Machine-Readable Agent Endpoints
items.article_standfirst: Every Gradio Space on Hugging Face now automatically exposes an `/agents` endpoint, allowing AI coding agents to discover and interact with the Space without manual configuration or wrapper code.
article_body: Hugging Face has updated its Gradio platform so that every Gradio Space now automatically serves an `/agents` endpoint at runtime. This endpoint provides a structured, machine-readable API description that any compatible AI agent can parse and call directly, eliminating the need for developers to write custom integration code or documentation parsers for each Space they want an agent to use.

The endpoint is specifically designed for AI coding agents such as Claude Code, Codex, and Pi. Rather than requiring a human developer to read a Space's README or manually construct API calls, an agent can point itself at the `/agents` endpoint and, according to Hugging Face, 'figure out how to use the Space without any setup.' The feature went live in mid-April 2026 and has attracted significant community interest, with over 150 upvotes on the changelog entry.

For developers building agent-based workflows, the change means that Gradio-hosted demos, tools, and UIs can now be seamlessly wired into autonomous coding agents without any per-Space integration work. This lowers the barrier for connecting third-party Gradio Spaces into agent toolchains and could accelerate the ecosystem of AI agents that work across the broader Hugging Face model and application hub.
article_why_matters: Agent authors no longer need to write custom wrappers or parse documentation to integrate a Gradio Space, cutting a significant friction point in building tool-calling pipelines.
The change positions Gradio Spaces as a discoverable, zero-config extension layer for AI coding agents, potentially expanding the audience for Space creators.
It establishes a standardized machine-readable interface convention on Hugging Face's platform, which could become a de facto pattern for other hosting providers.
article_source_note: This briefing is drawn from the official Hugging Face Changelog, specifically the entry dated April 17, 2026, describing the new `/agents.md` endpoint behavior for Gradio Spaces. The changelog is community-upvoted, confirming audience interest, but it does not include technical implementation details such as the endpoint schema format or which Gradio versions are required. Developers wanting to confirm compatibility or read the linked documentation should consult Hugging Face's official Gradio Spaces agent integration guide.

items.article_h1: DeepSeek API Consolidates to V4 Models, Sunsetting Legacy Chat and Reasoner Endpoints
items.article_standfirst: DeepSeek has announced that its legacy deepseek-chat and deepseek-reasoner model identifiers will be retired on July 24, 2026, consolidating its API lineup around deepseek-v4-flash and deepseek-v4-pro.
article_body: Developers using the DeepSeek API must update their model references before July 24, 2026, when deepseek-chat and deepseek-reasoner are officially deprecated. The deprecation was flagged in the company's official API documentation, which notes that deepseek-chat maps to the non-thinking mode of deepseek-v4-flash, while deepseek-reasoner maps to the thinking mode of the same base model. The retirement is part of a broader consolidation of DeepSeek's model portfolio around its newer V4 generation. The DeepSeek API maintains compatibility with both OpenAI and Anthropic SDKs, using a base URL of https://api.deepseek.com for OpenAI-format calls and https://api.deepseek.com/anthropic for Anthropic-format calls. Available models currently include deepseek-v4-flash, deepseek-v4-pro, and the two legacy identifiers pending retirement.
article_why_matters: Any application currently calling deepseek-chat or deepseek-reasoner will break after July 24, 2026, requiring code changes before that date.
The consolidation means developers need to explicitly choose between the fast, non-thinking deepseek-v4-flash and the more capable deepseek-v4-pro for their use cases.
Because DeepSeek's API format mirrors OpenAI and Anthropic conventions, migration is straightforward — the primary change is swapping model identifier strings.
article_source_note: Information drawn from DeepSeek's official API documentation at api-docs.deepseek.com, which specifies the deprecation timeline, base URLs, and current model lineup. Developers should monitor the same documentation for any updates to the July 24, 2026 cutoff date.
